digest.top_line: NRG-GU005: 5-fx prostate SBRT not superior to MH-IMRT for DFS (HR 1.38, futility), but bowel-QoL MCID 35% vs 44% (p=0.034) favors SBRT.
digest.tldr: Prostate SBRT carried the day. NRG-GU005 (phase III) missed DFS superiority over moderate hypofrac but delivered less bowel/GU/incontinence QoL decline, echoed by a 10-yr nonrandomized series (90% RFS, late G3 GI/GU ≤1.5%, no ADT). Two consensus docs round it out: ARS AUC for intraprostatic radiorecurrence salvage and ESTRO post-op oral-cavity CTV delineation.

sites.intro: First ESTRO consensus method for post-op oral-cavity CTV delineation, aimed at the wide institutional variation in PORT volumes.

studies.name: ESTRO Post-op Oral Cavity CTV Guidelines
studies.tldr: Post-op primary CTV-P = (GTV-P pre-op +10mm) composited with (surgical defect/flap +5mm); first consensus method to standardize oral-cavity PORT delineation.
details: 🔍 First ESTRO consensus guideline for post-op CTV delineation in oral cavity SCC (OCSCC); fills a gap driving wide individual/institutional/national variation in PORT volumes

details.text: 🔍 CTV-P construction (composite of two surrogate expansions)
subdetails: Re-create surrogate GTV-P pre-op on planning CT via diagnostic MRI/CT registration or anatomical landmarks
CTV-P = union of both, edited for air, bone, teeth and anatomical barriers

details: 📐 Nodal: CTV-N = surrogate GTV-N + involved level (CTV-N1); separate prophylactic-dose CTV-N2 for at-risk levels, plus uni-/bilateral neck decision rules

details.text: 💊 Prescription in worked example: PTV/CTV-P EQD2 60Gy (2Gy fractions)

details: 🔗 Adapts the 2018 definitive-RT '5mm+5mm' geometric-expansion CTV concept to the post-op setting where no residual GTV remains
🔗 DAHANCA: geometric (vs anatomical) GTV→CTV margins yield more conformal CTVs, less open to misinterpretation
🔗 Anatomical→geometric margin switch correlated with lower late dysphagia (unplanned post-hoc analysis)
⚠️ Consensus expert opinion, not outcome-validated; geometric margins not tested against recurrence patterns or a local-control endpoint
studies.significance: The contouring change is dual-surrogate: build CTV-P from BOTH the re-created pre-op GTV-P (+10mm) AND the surgical defect/flap (+5mm), not the defect alone, which the buccal case shows misses superiorly (infratemporal fossa). Geometric margins beat anatomical for conformality and late dysphagia (DAHANCA); example dose stays EQD2 60Gy.
studies.monday_clinic: In resected oral cavity SCC headed to PORT, this standardizes CTV-P as a geometric composite (GTV-P pre-op +10mm with surgical defect/flap +5mm); it does not extend to other H&N subsites or to definitive, non-post-op RT.
open_questions: Extension to other head/neck subsites (larynx, oropharynx, hypopharynx)
Validation of geometric margins against recurrence patterns
Impact on inter-observer variability in prospective audit

sites.intro: Three radiation-forward reads: a phase III hypofractionation QoL readout, 10-yr SBRT durability data, and a salvage-reirradiation AUC for radiorecurrence.

studies.name: ARS AUC: Intraprostatic Recurrence After RT
studies.tldr: Consensus AUC: PSMA PET + mpMRI + confirmatory biopsy before salvage reirradiation; short classic ADT preferred over ARSI, most regimens ≤6 fractions.
details: 🔍 ARS GU Appropriate Use Criteria consensus (modified Delphi, RAND); four topics across workup + local salvage of intraprostatic radiorecurrence
🔍 Workup: PSMA PET + mpMRI to exclude mets and map local extent; biopsy required before salvage (radiographic recurrence may be treatment effect)

details.text: 📊 FORECAST — mpMRI vs MRI-targeted biopsy for radiorecurrence detection (systematic biopsy reference)

details: 📊 PSMA PET restaging (Liu, n=79 exclusively radiorecurrent): conventional vs PET concordant 56%, 34% upstaged, 10% downstaged

details.text: 📊 RTOG 9408 post-hoc (n=831 post-RT biopsy): positive biopsy → higher BCR, HR 1.7, independent of upfront ADT

details: 💊 Short-course classic ADT preferred over novel hormonal agents (ARSI) for radiosensitization when salvage RT pursued
📊 Local salvage preferred over noncurative hormonal manipulation alone; most reirradiation regimens ≤6 fractions
🔍 Focal salvage when focal recurrence confirmed via multiple imaging + tissue modalities; whole-gland salvage toxicity called very tolerable
⚠️ Evidence limited to conventionally fractionated EBRT and pre-PSMA-PET-era workup; prospective data awaited
studies.significance: The operational read: confirmatory biopsy is mandatory before salvage (radiographic recurrence can be pure treatment effect), classic short-course ADT beats ARSI for radiosensitization, and whole-gland salvage toxicity is called very tolerable with most regimens ≤6 fractions. Caveat: evidence is conventional-EBRT-only, pre-PSMA-PET era, so transfer to post-SBRT recurrence is untested.
studies.monday_clinic: In BCR after definitive conventional EBRT with PSMA-PET/mpMRI-confirmed intraprostatic-only recurrence and positive biopsy, this supports curative local salvage over ADT alone; it does not extend to recurrences after primary SBRT.
open_questions: Optimal salvage reirradiation modality: brachytherapy vs SBRT vs re-EBRT
Salvage outcomes after primary SBRT or hypofractionation, not just conventional EBRT
Focal vs whole-gland salvage: comparative toxicity and local control

related_trials.brief_title: Stereotactic Re-irradiation of Local Recurrences of Prostate Cancer After Radiotherapy
related_trials.brief_summary:
The goal of this clinical study is to evaluate the toxicity and efficacy of re-irradiation using focal stereotactic body radiotherapy (SBRT) in patients with local recurrence of prostate cancer after definitive or post-operative radiotherapy.

The main question is the tolerance of such treatment, concerning the incidence of Grade ≥ 2 and Grade ≥ 3 GU and GI toxicity. Also the efficacy of SBRT will be measured in terms of Biochemical Control with other secondary endpoints which include:…
conditions: Local Recurrence of Malignant Tumor of Prostate
related_trials.eligibility_brief:
Inclusion Criteria:

1. Local recurrence of prostate cancer after definitive radiotherapy

   1. biopsy proven or/and
   2. Consistent MRI and PET-PSMA results and PSA growth dynamics
2. Time since primary radiotherapy - at least 2 years
3. Good performance status (ZUBROD 0-1)

   * If the results…
related_trials.answers_question: Optimal salvage reirradiation modality: brachytherapy vs SBRT vs re-EBRT
related_trials.relevance_phrase: phase 2 focal SBRT reirradiation, GU/GI toxicity

related_trials.brief_title: Partial Prostate Salvage High Dose Rate Brachytherapy
related_trials.brief_summary: A dose-response relationship for radiation in the management of prostate cancer is well established. Local recurrence of prostate cancer after external beam radiotherapy occurs in at least 40% of patients treated because of inability to deliver sufficient dose through external beam techniques. These patients respond well to re-irradiation using brachytherapy with about 50% of selected patients remaining free of recurrence 5 years after salvage. Advanced imaging using multiparametric Magnetic…
related_trials.eligibility_brief:
Inclusion Criteria:

* Age \>45 and Life expectancy \>10 years
* Previous External Beam Radiotherapy (EBRT) or LDR brachytherapy w
* \> 3 year interval since EBRT or LDR Brachytherapy
* No late toxicity from prior EBRT ≥ grade 2
* PSA \> nadir + 2 ng/ml and \< 10 ng/ml
* PSA Doubling time \> 6…
related_trials.answers_question: Optimal salvage reirradiation modality: brachytherapy vs SBRT vs re-EBRT
related_trials.relevance_phrase: salvage HDR brachytherapy after prior EBRT/LDR

studies.name: 10-yr SBRT for Prostate Cancer (Nonrandomized Trial)
studies.tldr: 10-yr RFS 90% (94% LR, 86% IR), OS 84%, late G3+ GI/GU ≤1.5% after 40Gy/5fx prostate SBRT, no ADT.
details: 📊 10-yr relapse-free survival 90% overall
📊 10-yr overall survival 84%
🔍 Phase 2 nonrandomized, N=310 (172 low-risk, 138 intermediate-risk) across 21 centers, no ADT
🔍 Median follow-up 9 yr, median age 68; central pathologic review
📐 40 Gy/5fx (8 Gy ×5, ≈100 Gy EQD2 at a/b=2), noncoplanar robotic platform, real-time motion tracking
🔗 PACE-B: SBRT noninferior to conventional EBRT at 5 yr; this extends durability to 10 yr
⚠️ Single-arm, no randomized comparator; risk-group RFS vs historical EBRT only

verdict.rationale: Single-arm nonrandomized phase 2, no within-trial comparator (historical EBRT only); 10-yr follow-up mature but design precludes a confirmatory read.
verdict.audience: Low/intermediate-risk localized prostate, Gleason ≤7, no prior ADT

studies.significance: The unfavorable-IR read is the additive one: 10-yr RFS 77% vs 92% in favorable IR, below the 86% pooled IR figure. With no ADT and 40Gy/5fx (≈100Gy EQD2), this backs SBRT monotherapy for favorable IR but leaves unfavorable IR as the subgroup where durability without intensification stays open.
studies.monday_clinic: In low-risk or favorable intermediate-risk localized prostate choosing ultrahypofractionated SBRT, this adds 10-yr durability (RFS ≥92%) and low late-toxicity data; it does not inform unfavorable-IR, high-risk, or gland >100cc patients.
open_questions: Durability in unfavorable intermediate-risk without ADT
10-yr randomized SBRT vs conventional EBRT comparison
Whether ADT adds benefit for intermediate-risk prostate SBRT

related_trials.brief_title: A Study of Stereotactic Body Radiation Therapy (SBRT) Without Androgen Deprivation Therapy (ADT) in People With Unfavorable Intermediate-Risk Prostate Cancer
related_trials.brief_summary: The researchers are doing this study to find out if stereotactic body radiation therapy (SBRT) without androgen deprivation therapy (ADT) is an effective treatment approach for people with unfavorable intermediate-risk prostate. The researchers will see whether SBRT can prevent participants' cancer from coming back and/or spreading to other parts of the body. In addition, they will look closely at how safe and effective it is to rely on Decipher test results for determining which patients would…

Inclusion Criteria:

* Pathologically proven diagnosis of prostate adenocarcinoma within 12 months of enrollment
* Unfavorable intermediate risk prostate cancer by NCCN 2021 risk stratification guidelines, including any of the following clinicopathologic features:
* Gleason Score 4+3
* ≥ 50% biopsy…

related_trials.brief_summary: Accelerated Hypofractionation Radiotherapy for prostate cancer of 36.25 Gy delivered in 5 fractions will not be inferior to the standard treatment of 70.2 Gy given in 26 fractions with respect to four-year biochemical failure (PSA failure) by Phoenix definition post-treatment completion.

related_trials.answers_question: 10-yr randomized SBRT vs conventional EBRT comparison

studies.name: NRG-GU005
studies.tldr: Bowel QoL MCID decline 35% vs 44% (p=0.034) favoring 5-fx SBRT; but DFS superiority not met (HR 1.38, futility) vs MH-IMRT.

details.text: 🔍 Randomized non-blinded phase III, N=698 (353 SBRT, 345 MH-IMRT), localized intermediate-risk; co-primaries DFS + EPIC-26 bowel/urinary-irritation MCID at 24mo

subdetails: SBRT 36.25 Gy/5 fx (7.25 Gy/fx, 2-3×/wk); MH-IMRT 70 Gy/28 fx or 60 Gy/20 fx
SBRT CTV-PTV 5mm radial, 3mm posterior/anterior; MH-IMRT 8mm except 5mm posterior
Fiducials + MRI fusion + daily IGRT mandated

details: 📊 DFS co-primary superiority NOT met: interim futility bound crossed; SBRT not superior to MH-IMRT for disease-free survival
📊 Longitudinal bowel domain favored SBRT: LS mean 2.68 (95% CI 1.02-4.34, p=0.0016)
📊 Co-primary QoL split: bowel MCID met (favors SBRT), urinary irritation/obstruction MCID not met (1 of 2 positive)

details.text: 📊 EPIC-26 MCID decline by domain, SBRT vs MH-IMRT

details: 🔗 vs PACE-B (other phase III of SBRT vs MH-IMRT): both trials show lower urinary-incontinence QoL decline with SBRT
⚠️ Superiority (not non-inferiority) design: futility means SBRT unproven-superior, not established equivalent

details.text: ⚠️ Rectal spacer used ~55% both arms, independently improved EPIC bowel scores (LS mean -2.81, 95% CI -4.49 to -1.13, p=0.0011); partial confounder for bowel-QoL benefit

verdict.rationale: Phase III RCT with mature f/u; SBRT already guideline-endorsed. Bowel-QoL co-primary met, DFS superiority not; result reinforces SBRT as an accepted option, not a new standard.
verdict.audience: Localized intermediate-risk prostate cancer, median age 68

studies.significance: The subtle signal under the QoL win: 3y biochemical failure ran higher with SBRT (8% vs 4%, p=0.037), though 5y DFS and OS were comparable. Net, 5-fraction SBRT holds oncologic control while cutting bowel-QoL decline and G≥3 GU toxicity (0.6% vs 2.5%), supporting it over 20-28 fraction MH-IMRT for intermediate-risk prostate.
studies.monday_clinic: In localized intermediate-risk prostate cancer weighing fractionation, this supports 5-fraction SBRT as oncologically comparable with a bowel-QoL edge; it does not speak to high-risk disease or elective nodal RT, both outside the enrolled prostate-only volume.
open_questions: Durability of higher SBRT biochemical-failure signal with longer follow-up
SBRT bowel-QoL benefit independent of rectal spacer use

arms.label: MH-IMRT (70 Gy/28 fx or 60 Gy/20 fx)

related_trials.brief_summary: Patients with prostate cancer and pelvic lymph node involvement (cN1M0) identified on PSMA PET imaging represent a biologically aggressive yet potentially curable disease population. Contemporary management relies on multimodality treatment combining definitive radiotherapy to the prostate and pelvic lymph nodes with long-term androgen deprivation therapy (ADT), often intensified with androgen receptor pathway inhibitors. Despite these advances, a substantial proportion of patients still…

related_trials.brief_title: Testing Shorter Duration Radiation Therapy Versus the Usual Radiation Therapy in Patients With High Risk Prostate Cancer
related_trials.brief_summary: This phase III trial compares stereotactic body radiation therapy (SBRT), (five treatments over two weeks using a higher dose per treatment) to usual radiation therapy (20 to 45 treatments over 4 to 9 weeks) for the treatment of high-risk prostate cancer. SBRT uses special equipment to position a patient and deliver radiation to tumors with high precision. This method may kill tumor cells with fewer doses over a shorter period of time. This trial is evaluating if shorter duration radiation…
